behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a pipeline handoff check (inventory filter vs ranking), not just one function. REAL-INFRA/SYSTEM TRIAD not required by text (no real HTTP/subprocess/external contract/explicit round-trip external side effect). LLM-DEP rejected: behavior text describes internal weighting/filtering logic, not a real AI/ML API/model-quality assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation service end-to-end in-process with seeded catalog + stock flags + relevance inputs; assert out-of-stock items never appear in returned ranked results.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires user-settings module + filtering module wiring. No explicit real OS/network/subprocess need. LLM-DEP rejected by behavior text: deterministic settings-based exclusion, no model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user profile with blocked categories, run recommendation generation through service + repository layers, and assert blocked categories are absent.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ranking depends on combining browsing-signal extraction and scorer/ranker wiring. System rejected: behavior does not require external contract checks. Agentic considered but rejected because behavior text specifies a recommendation algorithm using internal signals, not an explicit real AI/ML API call or non-deterministic model output requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Seed browsing history and candidate products with/without matching signals, execute full ranking path, and assert matching-signal products rank above no-signal products.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires ranking output, sorting stability, and pagination module interaction. REAL-INFRA/SYSTEM TRIAD not explicit. No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed seeded data and unchanged state, request page 2 twice via recommendation service API boundary (in-process) and assert identical ordered IDs and page window correctness.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: serendipity rule is an interaction between exploration logic and rank/filter pipeline. System rejected (no explicit external infra contract). Agentic considered but rejected due missing explicit real AI/ML model call in behavior text; this can be validated as deterministic business logic if implemented that way.
behaviors.test_description: Seed user history concentrated in a subset of categories and run full recommendation generation; assert at least one top-N recommendation comes from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: cold-start fallback needs interaction between user-state detection and popularity source/ranker. No explicit need for real external infra. LLM-DEP rejected: popularity fallback is deterministic business rule in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with zero history, run recommendation generation through service/repo layers, and assert non-empty results align with popularity ordering.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: needs interaction between recommendation response metadata and tracking write path. System considered, but behavior text does not explicitly require a real external analytics service contract; local persistence round-trip is sufficient for this stated behavior. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, simulate click events, persist tracking records, then read back stored events and assert product ID/position/timestamp fields match emitted clicks.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: performance target is only meaningful across full module composition, not single-function units. System considered, but behavior text does not require real network/subprocess/external contract checks; an in-process end-to-end benchmark can provide the needed additional signal. No AI dependency stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process performance test on seeded 100k catalog through the full recommendation pipeline and assert p95 (or specified metric) remains under 200ms under controlled test conditions.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires state handoff across purchase event ingestion and subsequent ranking. System rejected: no explicit external contract or real infra requirement in behavior text. Agentic considered but rejected because behavior can be validated as deterministic reprioritization logic without explicit model-output-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Issue recommendations, record a purchase for one recommended product, request recommendations again, and assert the purchased item rank decreases (or is suppressed) per policy.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: determinism must be checked across full pipeline (feature fetch, ranking, tie-break, pagination/ordering), not isolated units. System rejected: no explicit real external contract needed. Agentic rejected by text unless implementation uses non-deterministic model calls, which is not specified here.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed user state and fixed data snapshot, invoke recommendation generation twice through the service boundary and assert identical ranked product IDs in order.
